key_points: International students must pay $13K room and board upfront
On-campus jobs available but capped at 20 hrs/week — unlikely to cover costs
No specific praise or complaints about dorm quality found
key_quotes: I am required to pay 13K for R&B, I have a scholarship which waives tuition
You can work only after you have arrived on campus and 20 hrs weekly max

key_points: XULA's small size is 'a blessing and a curse' — easy to adjust, easy to feel trapped
92% Black undergraduate population at XULA creates a tight-knit but insular community
Xavier Cincinnati has regional Midwest recognition but limited national social buzz
key_quotes: The school's small size has become a blessing and a curse for me
Being a minority in any group is a pretty lonely existence even if you aren't ostracized

key_points: No student reviews mention dining halls at either Xavier institution
No complaints or praise surfaced in any Reddit or YouTube data
Absence of data suggests dining is neither a selling point nor a crisis
key_quotes: No direct quotes available on dining from either source

key_points: One student reports Xavier Cincinnati mismanaged their medications during a mental health crisis
Administration handling of medical situations led to academic collapse and years-long dropout
No evidence of robust mental health support systems mentioned by any student
key_quotes: I tried to kill myself due to them messing with my medications and not knowing I had a brain tumor
My grades second semester went downhill because of said medication cocktail and I decided to take a year off

key_points: XULA's Community-Engaged Fellowship replaces existing merit aid — could be a downgrade
Xavier Cincinnati offers merit scholarships that outcompete even NYU offers
XULA students on tight budgets warned: on-campus jobs alone cannot realistically cover room and board
key_quotes: The scholarship awards 30k every year, which replaces your original merit scholarship. Doesn't seem worth it to me with the 10 hours
Xavier gave me a merit scholarship while NYU didn't give me a single dime and expects me to pay 90k per year

key_points: XULA is the #2 producer of Black medical school applicants nationally — pre-med reputation is real
XULA's Biomedical Honors Corps and early assurance with Tulane Medical School are standout programs
Xavier Cincinnati is respected regionally in Midwest but alumni question national employer recognition
key_quotes: Xula is a much bigger school for pre-med. Three times as many med school applicants, even though it is a much smaller school
Xavier University has an early assurance program with Tulane's medical school
Xavier seems to be respected regionally in the Midwest. Is it respected nationally by employers as well?

key_points: XULA freshman cites 'glaring problems with school systems' as reason to transfer
Xavier Cincinnati administration has a documented case of medication mismanagement causing serious harm
Scholarship details are opaque — students can't find fellowship amounts on the official website
key_quotes: A lot of problems with the schools systems and general campus itself have become glaring for me
It does not specify how much and I can't find it anywhere
I tried to kill myself due to them messing with my medications

key_points: XULA is in New Orleans — a major cultural draw but a city with real safety considerations
Xavier Cincinnati sits in a mid-sized Midwest city with solid regional connections
Small campus size at XULA means limited anonymity and fewer on-campus resources

The school's small size has become a blessing and a curse for me

Xavier Cincinnati alumni described as 'impressive humans' but national employer reach is unconfirmed

key_quotes: Xula is a much bigger school for pre-med. Second largest source of black med school applicants in the country

key_points: XULA can be cost-competitive but room and board costs are a serious burden without family support
Xavier Cincinnati merit scholarships are aggressive — one student chose it over NYU for financial reasons
For pre-med, multiple Reddit users say go wherever is cheapest — prestige matters less than GPA

THE QUALITY OF PRE-MED CLASSES IS GOING TO BE ESSENTIALLY THE SAME WHETHER YOU GO TO COMMUNITY COLLEGE OR A PRESTIGIOUS UNIVERSITY

key_points: XULA has a strong mission-driven community identity centered on Black excellence and pre-med success
Xavier Cincinnati has a loyal but quieter fanbase — more Jesuit academic culture than party school
Both schools attract students who are serious about goals, not social prestige
key_quotes: I want to be around my community being a Black woman
